report_name: Healthcare Under Siege: Ransomware Attacks Jump 14%, Now Targeting Supply Chain

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: Identified as the most prolific ransomware group perpetrating attacks against the healthcare sector and its supply chain in the first half of 2026.

threat_actors.name: DragonForce
threat_actors.description: Mentioned as one of the active ransomware groups targeting the healthcare sector in H1 2026.

threat_actors.name: Kairos
threat_actors.description: Mentioned as one of the active ransomware groups targeting the healthcare sector in H1 2026.

threat_actors.name: INC
threat_actors.description: Mentioned as one of the active ransomware groups targeting the healthcare sector in H1 2026.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used as a common initial access vector in ransomware attacks against the healthcare sector.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Exploitation of unpatched vulnerabilities in internet-facing systems like VPNs or RDP is used for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Network Service Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Attackers perform network reconnaissance to identify high-value targets like electronic health record (EHR) databases and file servers.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Used by attackers for lateral movement within compromised healthcare networks.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: Patient and organizational data is exfiltrated to attacker-controlled servers, often in the cloud, before encryption as part of a double-extortion model.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The final stage of the attack where a ransomware payload is deployed to encrypt data on systems like EHR databases and file servers.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use utilities like vssadmin to delete volume shadow copies, preventing easy restoration of encrypted files from local backups.

malware.name: Qilin Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload deployed by the Qilin group to encrypt data on victim systems in the healthcare sector.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: The Volume Shadow Copy Service utility is used by attackers to delete backups as a precursor to encryption, inhibiting system recovery.

tools.name: BITSAdmin
tools.description: Mentioned as a legitimate tool that could be used suspiciously for downloading malicious payloads or exfiltrating data.

identities.name: Healthcare Sector
identities.description: The global healthcare sector, including hospitals and clinics, which saw a 14% increase in ransomware attacks in H1 2026.

identities.name: Healthcare Supply Chain
identities.description: Business associates and suppliers to the healthcare industry, such as pharmaceutical companies, medical billing firms, and technology providers, which are increasingly targeted by ransomware groups.

identities.name: Unimed
identities.description: A German medical billing service that suffered a data breach affecting 135,000 individuals due to a ransomware attack, highlighting the impact of supply chain targeting.

identities.name: Japanese medical institution
identities.description: An unnamed medical institution in Japan that received a massive $100 million ransom demand.

infrastructure.name: Attacker-controlled exfiltration servers
infrastructure.description: Servers, often cloud-based, used by ransomware groups to stage and exfiltrate victim data before encryption.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8f6d5c-1a3e-4b9f-8c7d-9e0a1b2c3d4e
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common precursor to ransomware encryption.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/ransomware/healthcare-ransomware-attacks-remain-resilient-in-h1-2026-as-extortion-groups-broaden-attacks-across-supply-chain/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-11
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of the vssadmin utility to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP to inhibit system recovery.

indicators.name: Ransomware Note Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Ransomware Note Creation
id: f5a4b3c2-d1e0-4987-a6b5-c4d3e2f1a0b9
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with names commonly used for ransom notes.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/ransomware/healthcare-ransomware-attacks-remain-resilient-in-h1-2026-as-extortion-groups-broaden-attacks-across-supply-chain/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-11
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: 'README'
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.txt'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of text files with names like 'README.txt', which are commonly dropped by ransomware after encryption.
